audit_items.proof_trace: At line 623, realloc() result is assigned directly to slh->extra_list; line 624 immediately dereferences slh->extra_list[old_sz] with no NULL check. If realloc fails and returns NULL, the original pointer is overwritten with NULL (memory leak) and the subsequent array access is a NULL pointer dereference. No caller (ur_addr_map_put, add_socket_to_map) adds a compensating NULL check.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An external TURN client opens enough concurrent allocations or permissions to exhaust the ADDR_ARRAY_SIZE inline slots, forcing addr_list_add into the extra_list growth path; under memory pressure realloc returns NULL, triggering a NULL dereference crash (denial of service).
